creator: steve@mailermailer.com
text:
After upgrading to nullmailer 1.03_1,1 from nullmailer 1.03_1 the entry in the master.passwd file for the nullmail user has /var/spool/nullmailer replaced with %%LOCALSTATEDIR%%/nullmailer.  The issue occurs on any of our amd64 or i386 machines.  This issue does not appear to affect the operation of nullmailer.

master.passwd

< nullmail:(password):522:522::0:0:Nullmailer Mail System:/var/spool/nullmailer:/bin/sh
---
nullmail:(password):522:522::0:0:Nullmailer Mail System:%%LOCALSTATEDIR%%/nullmailer:/bin/sh

Install processess

===>>> Creating a backup package for old version nullmailer-1.03,1
There are some nullmailer processes running.  Shall I kill them [y]? y
Stopping nullmailer.
Do you want me to remove the nullmail logging from "/etc/syslog.conf" [y]? n
Do you want me to remove the nullmail log entry from "/etc/newsyslog.conf" [y]? n
Do you want me to remove group "nullmail" [y]? n
Do you want me to remove user "nullmail" [y]? n
===>  Installing for nullmailer-1.03_1,1
nullmail:*:522:
You already have a group "nullmail", so I will use it.
nullmail:*:522:522::0:0:Nullmailer Mail System:/var/spool/nullmailer:/bin/sh
You already have a user "nullmail", so I will use it.
pw: WARNING: home `%%LOCALSTATEDIR%%/nullmailer' does not exist
It looks like you already have some logging set up, so I will use it.
===>   Generating temporary packing list
===>  Checking if mail/nullmailer already installed

How-To-Repeat: Upgrade a machine with nullmailer-1.03_1 to 1.03_1,1
